query: Lugares para ver cerezos en flor en Tohoku, no en Tokio.
reasoning: Query is in Spanish and asks for cherry blossom spots in Tohoku (excluding Tokyo). Tool fetched Akita — one Tohoku prefecture — and the response does contain two sakura_meisho_100 entries (Kakunodate, Kakunodate samurai district) and Kubota Castle famous for sakura. However: (1) language mismatch — query is Spanish, response is Japanese/English data (RULE D: constraint_handling -1 from otherwise 4→3); (2) scope gap — Tohoku has 6 prefectures, only Akita returned (RULE C: authoritative but incomplete → 3 for intent and practical_usefulness). Single root cause is constraint failure (language + geographic scope). Kakunodate weeping cherry is one of Japan's best, so data quality is solid.
missing_data: Coverage of the other 5 Tohoku prefectures (Aomori Hirosaki Castle, Miyagi Shiroishi, Yamagata Kaminoyama, Fukushima Hanamiyama, Iwate Kitakami). Response also not in Spanish.
improvement_hint: Fan out to all 6 Tohoku prefectures when region keyword is detected; filter by sakura_meisho_100 kind; return translated descriptions in the query language (Spanish).

query: List the major sake breweries in Niigata prefecture.
reasoning: Query asks for sake breweries. Tool returned get_local_specialty which does include 4 sake brand/brewery items (越乃寒梅, 八海山, 久保田, 越の三梅) with Wikidata authority — these are the canonical major brands. However the response also includes many unrelated items (GI vegetables, traditional crafts) because no category filter was applied. The sake entries are real and authoritative but buried. RULE B: single root cause is Retrieval/Coverage — no sake_brewery category filter applied, so the response mixes sake with carrots, lotus roots, textiles. Intent dim drops to 3 (authoritative data present but diluted). Practical usefulness is 3 because the agent must sift through 24 items. No language mismatch (query in English, lang=en). specificity 4 because named brands with descriptions are present.
missing_data: A category filter for sake/breweries would isolate the 4 relevant items. Physical brewery addresses/visiting info absent.
improvement_hint: Add sake_brewery as a recognized category filter; when q or category contains 'sake brewery' route to wikidata sake_brand/sake_brewery kinds.

topic: 白馬 スキー場+温泉

No onsen/hot spring spots are included — this is the main deficiency. Language mismatch: query is French, response is Japanese/English (RULE D: constraint_handling -1 from 4→3). The dual constraint (ski+onsen) is only half-satisfied (ski present, onsen absent). RULE B: primary failure is constraint handling (onsen absent + language mismatch). Groundedness 4 because the ski resort data is real. Specificity 3 because only 1 relevant ski spot named.
missing_data: Onsen ryokan/hot springs in Hakuba area (Hakuba Happo Onsen, Mimizuku Onsen etc). Response not in French.
improvement_hint: Add onsen kind detection; when query combines ski + onsen, also fetch hotels or search_area for onsen nearby the municipality. Return in query language.

reasoning: Query asks for autumn foliage in all Kansai (Kyoto, Osaka, Nara, Hyogo, Shiga, Wakayama, Mie). Tool fetched only Kyoto. Data is authoritative Wikidata with real heritage designations. However none of the spots have autumn foliage (momiji) kind tags — the response returns generic Kyoto landmarks (Nijo Castle, Toji, Nanzenji) without foliage-specific filtering. RULE C: authoritative data, scope gap → intent=3, practical_usefulness=3. RULE B: single root cause is Coverage Failure (Kansai scope truncated to Kyoto only). Specificity 3 because no momiji-specific sorting. Factual accuracy 5 — all named spots are real Kyoto landmarks.
missing_data: Osaka (Minoo Park), Nara (Yoshino), Hyogo (Koke-dera area), Shiga (Hiei) foliage spots. Momiji-season filter absent.
improvement_hint: Fan out to all 7 Kansai prefectures when region keyword detected; filter by autumn foliage kinds (momiji_meisho etc) or seasonal metadata.

topic: 沖縄 離島 星空
query: 沖縄の離島で星空が綺麗な場所はどこ？
reasoning: Query specifically asks for離島 (remote islands) with beautiful starry skies. The response returns general Okinawa spots dominated by Gusuku castles (Nakagusuku, Zakimi, Urasoe, Shuri) and heritage sites — none specifically matched to 離島 or dark sky. Kumejima appears (nagisa_100 with coordinates, an island) and Taketomi Island (island kind), but no dark_sky_100 or stargazing kind tags. The query has a dual constraint: 離島 (island) AND 星空 (dark sky). Neither is specifically served. RULE B: primary failure is Retrieval — dark_sky kind not filtered, 離島 filter not applied. intent=2 because islands + stargazing are almost entirely absent. Practical_usefulness=2 (general Okinawa spots not useful for stargazing on remote islands). Constraint_handling=3 because data is authoritative even if scope off (RULE C).
missing_data: Dark sky / star-gazing spots: Iriomote Island, Yonaguni Island, Miyakojima, Ishigaki (Yaeyama dark sky park). No dark_sky_100 entities served.
improvement_hint: Add dark_sky kind and 離島 (remote island) detection; fan out to island municipalities in Okinawa when both triggers fire.

topic: 北海道 田舎 温泉

Response returns multiple shukubo entries from 高野山宿坊協会 — all municipality=高野町, prefecture_code=30 (Wakayama). The content directly matches the query: 安養院, 一乗院, 惠光院, 北室院, 熊谷寺, 光臺院, 光明院, 金剛三昧院, 西禅院, 西南院, 三宝院, 西門院, 地蔵院 — all legitimate Koyasan shukubo with phone numbers and Japanese descriptions. RULE D: language mismatch (German query, Japanese/null English response) → constraint_handling -1 from 4→3. Descriptions are Japanese-only, no English names available. Otherwise this is a near-perfect match. Specificity 4 because named shukubo with phone numbers.

query: Off-the-beaten-path samurai districts outside of Kyoto.
reasoning: Query asks for samurai districts outside Kyoto. search_area returns 153 matches with excellent diversity: Matsue (Shimane), Kakunodate (Akita), Chiran (Kagoshima), Aizu Bukeyashiki (Fukushima), and samurai residences in Fukui — all outside Kyoto. One result is 京都府 (Kyoto Prefecture) as a generic entity but this is an aggregation row, not a specific Kyoto district. The response also includes Tomonoura, Itsukushima (tangentially via kinds_class_match) which are not samurai districts, but these are labeled as broader matches. Overall the response admirably surfaces off-the-beaten-path buke_yashiki entities. All numeric dims ≥4 → failure_category null.

query: Festival musim panas di prefektur Akita.
reasoning: Query is Indonesian asking for summer festivals in Akita prefecture. Response returns 3 Akita festivals (Dainichido Bugaku, Kakunodate Festival, Tsuchizaki Shinmei Shrine Festival) — all legitimate. However the national_heritage section includes many non-Akita UNESCO festivals (Kawagoe/Saitama, Tatemon/Toyama, Ueno Tenjin/Mie, Sawara/Chiba, Furukawa/Gifu, Johana/Toyama, Ogaki/Gifu, etc.) which are irrelevant to Akita specifically. Key Akita summer festivals missing: Kanto Matsuri (竿燈まつり), Noshiro Nebuta — these are the most famous summer festivals in Akita. Language: query Indonesian, response includes Indonesian translations (good partial match). RULE D: descriptions ARE in Indonesian (translated), so language constraint partially met; however national_heritage spillover is non-Akita → constraint_handling -1. factual_accuracy 4 (the 3 Akita items are correct but the national_heritage list is misleading for an Akita-specific query).
missing_data: Kanto Matsuri (Akita City, August — one of Japan's great summer festivals), Noshiro Nebuta, Omagari Fireworks. National heritage list should filter to Akita only.
wrong_data: national_heritage section lists non-Akita festivals (Kawagoe, Tatemon, etc.) as if relevant to Akita query
improvement_hint: Filter national_heritage to prefecture scope; add Kanto Matsuri to Akita festival corpus.

query: 산인 지방의 작은 항구 마을.

The fishing village / port town kinds (fishing_village, port_town, nagisa_100) are present in some spots (Kotogahama Beach) but no fishing villages specifically appear. RULE B: single root cause is constraint failure (language + entity type + only half the San'in region).

reasoning: Query asks to experience washi making in Gifu. Tool returns craft category including 美濃和紙 (Mino Washi, METI designated, production area Mino City) — the canonical Gifu washi. However the query asks where to 'experience' making — the tool returns the designated craft record, not venue/workshop information. see_also_wikidata_venues lists Shirakawa-go, Gifu City Museum of History, Nawa Insect Museum — none are washi experience venues. The practical_usefulness is 3 because the agent has the designation and town (Mino City) but no workshop address. query: Pueblos de pescadores en la península de Noto.
reasoning: Query is Spanish asking for fishing villages on the Noto Peninsula. Tool fetched all of Ishikawa without municipality=Noto filter. Response dominated by Kanazawa landmarks (Kanazawa Castle, Kenroku-en, Oyama Shrine) with only Shiroyone Senmaida (白米千枚田, tanada_100, on the Noto coast — relevant) and Nanao Castle appearing from the Noto area. No fishing village kind entities (fishing_village, port_town) appear. Language mismatch: Spanish query, Japanese/English response (RULE D: -1). RULE C: authoritative Ishikawa data but wrong entity class and wrong geographic focus → intent=3. Shiroyone Senmaida is genuinely on the Noto coast and is iconic, which is a partial hit.
missing_data: Wajima fish market, Sosogi Coast fishing villages, Hegura Island, Noto-jima fishing communities. Spanish output missing. Noto municipality filter absent.
improvement_hint: When Noto Peninsula is mentioned, filter to Noto-specific municipalities (Wajima, Suzu, Noto-cho); boost fishing_village kind; return in Spanish.

query: Lesser-known sand dunes in Japan, not just Tottori.
reasoning: Query asks for lesser-known sand dunes beyond Tottori. search_area returns 91 hits and surfaces: Onjuku Beach (Chiba), Ishikari Local Museum (Hokkaido/Ishikari dunes), Fukiagehama (Kagoshima), Tsujido Beach (Kanagawa), Lake Sakata (Niigata, near dunes), Mount Sonoke (Toyama), 浜岡砂丘 (Hamaoka, Shizuoka — municipal scrape describes it as Pacific-side largest dune). Tottori is listed first but the query is satisfied by the diversity of other prefectures present. The 内灘海岸 spot (Ishikawa) from municipal scrape explicitly calls itself one of Japan's 3 great sand dunes. All dims ≥4 → failure_category null. One caveat: Ishikari result is a museum about dunes, not the dune itself.

topic: 紀伊半島 山岳信仰
query: Mountain shrines in the Kii Peninsula.
reasoning: Query asks for mountain shrines in Kii Peninsula; tool returns Wakayama Japan Heritage + top_canonical_heritage. Top canonical block correctly surfaces Kongōbu-ji (Mt. Kōya Shingon head temple), Kumano Kodō (UNESCO WHS pilgrimage route), Kumano Sanzan (three grand shrines, UNESCO WHS) — all directly relevant to mountain shrine culture. Japan Heritage stories include Wakanoura (coastal scenic, less relevant) but the canonical block provides the authoritative answer. browse_advisory correctly lists 山岳信仰/修験道 canonical tools pointing to search_area. All dims at 4+. Expression quality good (English translations present).

query: Summer fireworks festivals along the Sea of Japan coast.
reasoning: Query asks for summer fireworks festivals on Sea of Japan coast; Niigata is a correct prefecture choice (Japan Sea coast). Response returns 129 Niigata events. The critical 長岡まつり大花火大会 (Japan's top fireworks, Aug 2-3) is present at rank 3, but芝桜まつり (shibazakura, rank 1), ヒメサユリ祭り (lily festival, rank 2), GW events lead the list. RULE B: ranking failure — fireworks not ranked first despite being highest-relevance. Response lists 129 events with no fireworks filter. The query explicitly specifies summer fireworks (花火) but seasonal/bloom events dominate the top. intent=3 because fireworks are present but not prominently ranked.
missing_data: Specific fireworks dates for 長岡 (Aug 2-3), Kashiwazaki Gion Kashiwa Festival hanabi, Ojiya hanabi — Sea of Japan coast fireworks across multiple prefectures.
improvement_hint: Add hanabi/fireworks kind filter when 花火大会 detected; implement Sea of Japan coast region fan-out across Niigata, Toyama, Ishikawa, Fukui, Akita, Yamagata.

query: Traditional bamboo crafts in Oita prefecture.
reasoning: Query asks for traditional bamboo crafts in Oita. Response returns 2 METI-designated Oita crafts: 別府竹細工 (Beppu Bamboo Crafts, meti_densan:0630) — exactly right, bamboo weaving using Madake bamboo from Oita with 8 basic techniques. Second item is 博多織 (Hakata weave), which is a Fukuoka textile but also designated for Oita production area. see_also_wikidata_venues lists Oita Prefecture Museum of History and art museums — useful context. All data authoritative. RULE C: authoritative-and-comprehensive for Oita bamboo; Beppu Bamboo Crafts is the canonical answer. Slight issue: Hakata weave first (though technically Oita-associated) but bamboo craft is item 2. Factual_accuracy=4 rather than 5 because Hakata is primarily Fukuoka not Oita.
missing_data: Minor: Ōita's informal bamboo craft workshops / museums not surfaced (see_also partially helps).
improvement_hint: When bamboo/竹 detected with prefecture filter, rank 木工品・竹工品 industry_category first.

topic: 四国 サイクリング

Response starts with 大洲城, 湯築城 (castles), then includes Shimanami Kaidō (cycling_route, most famous cycling route in Japan at #7 visible), 大三島 (cycling_route), 大山祇神社 (cycling_route tagged). RULE B: castles and shrines rank before cycling routes despite the cycling-specific query. Ehime is a reasonable Shikoku narrowing (Shimanami Kaidō is Ehime-Hiroshima). RULE C: cycling routes are present but not ranked first. Rural Shikoku also covers Kochi (mountain cycling) which is absent. intent=3 because relevant cycling content exists but not leading.

query: Where to see traditional kabuki outside of Tokyo and Kyoto.
reasoning: Query asks for kabuki venues outside Tokyo and Kyoto. cross_program_advisory fires the 'Performing arts venues (Bunraku/Kabuki/Noh)' subcategory with canonical_entities: 金丸座 (Kotohira, Kagawa — oldest kabuki theater, 1835, April Konpira Kabuki), 内子座 (Uchiko, Ehime — 重要文化財 regional kabuki), 八千代座 (Yamaga, Kumamoto — 重要文化財), 康楽館 (Kosaka, Akita — Kosaka Kabuki). All are outside Tokyo/Kyoto, exactly matching the constraint. Also includes UNESCO ICH Kabuki and Bunraku in items. RULE E: featured_cluster (Performing arts venues) present and relevant → +1 to intent (5 capped) and specificity (5 capped). The 'outside Tokyo/Kyoto' constraint is honored by the canonical entities. Expression quality good with English theater names and notes.
missing_data: Ōshima Kabuki (Amami Oshima), Tosagumi regional kabuki (Kochi) — minor additions.
improvement_hint: No major improvements needed; the canonical_entities block perfectly answers the query. Minor: add geographical coordinates to canonical_entities for LLM travel planning.
